route.name: NorCal Outback
route.description: Public GPX long Northern California/Southern Oregon backcountry route with gravel, forest roads, remote connectors, and sparse services.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX long Northern California/Southern Oregon backcountry route with gravel, forest roads, remote connectors, and sparse services.
route.bestSeason: Shoulder season or carefully screened summer windows; July needs heat, smoke, fire, and water checks.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX long Northern California/Southern Oregon backcountry route with gravel, forest roads, remote connectors, and sparse services.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Remote water/resupply gaps need measurement from the GPX and current source checks.
resources.food: Sparse town/service sequence; verify all hours and closures.
resources.camping: Multi-night camp sequence must be planned and verified before any promotion.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before ride-ready promotion.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Use as a future California/NorCal epic or segment candidate.
Do not promote the full route for July 10-13 without a segment plan.
Check fire closures and smoke first.
hazards: too long for a simple weekend

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control ride-ready promotion.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: segment_only; full 319-mile route does not fit July 10-13 and no shorter standalone segment is vetted
blockers: Not ride-ready until date-specific camp/permit, water, legal access, closures, fire/smoke, and weather are refreshed.
Full NorCal Outback is not a July 10-13 ride-ready route; at 319 miles over roughly 6 days it needs a separately vetted shorter segment before next-weekend use.
Full route is 319 miles / 6 days and does not fit July 10-13.
No shorter GPX-backed NorCal Outback segment has a verified July camp sequence, legal-access audit, water gap measurement, and final smoke/weather check.
Klamath Very High fire danger and Modoc High fire danger require segment-level final refresh.
requiredFixes: Complete July 9-10 final refresh for next-weekend ride readiness.
Choose a short July 10-13 NorCal Outback segment rather than treating the full route as usable.
Measure water and resupply gaps from the selected GPX segment.
Verify segment-level road/trail legality, camping rules, and Modoc/Klamath fire/smoke/closure status within 24-48 hours of departure.
Select one explicit GPX segment and export it as a standalone artifact before promotion.
Verify all roads/trails for the selected segment against relevant land managers.
Confirm July 10-13 camps or lodging and all water/resupply gaps.
Refresh CAL FIRE, AirNow/Fire and Smoke Map, and NWS within 24-48 hours of departure.
openQuestions: Do current closures/fire/weather alter the imported GPX?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: Imported route metadata lists the full NorCal Outback at approximately 319 miles and 6 days, which does not fit a July 10-13 next-weekend trip without reducing it to a separately vetted segment.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Official/public GPX has been imported and parsed locally.
promotionChecklist.campingPermitsNote: Multi-night camp sequence is not selected or verified. Modoc camping alert lists 14-day/group-size limits, but its forest-order text has stale effective dates and must be refreshed with the district before relying on it.
promotionChecklist.closuresFireWeatherNote: Klamath lists Very High fire danger in both East and West zones; Modoc lists High fire danger even though current Modoc fire restrictions page says no restrictions are in place. Fire, smoke, and closure checks must be refreshed before choosing a shorter segment.
promotionChecklist.legalAccessNote: The route crosses multiple land managers and forest districts; GPX-level road/trail legality and current closures must be checked for any selected segment.
promotionChecklist.waterResupplyNote: Remote Modoc/Klamath gaps have not been measured against the GPX and current water availability has not been verified.

days.name: NorCal Outback segment 1
days.camp: Multi-night camp sequence must be planned and verified before any promotion.

waypoints.name: J and J Grizzly General Store

logistics: Final ride-ready status requires live date-specific resource and legal checks.
Modoc National Forest fire restriction source checked July 2, 2026: https://www.fs.usda.gov/r05/modoc/alerts/forest-fire-restrictions
Modoc National Forest camping restriction source checked July 2, 2026: https://www.fs.usda.gov/r05/modoc/alerts/camping-restrictions-0
Klamath National Forest source checked July 2, 2026: https://www.fs.usda.gov/r05/klamath

readinessEvidence.finding: Imported public GPX route metadata identifies NorCal Outback as a 319-mile, roughly 6-day route; the full route does not fit a next-weekend window.

Modoc National Forest lists High fire danger. Its fire restriction alert says no current fire restrictions are in place, but conditions remain dry and California Campfire Permits are still required for campfires.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Modoc National Forest Forest Fire Restrictions alert

readinessEvidence.finding: Klamath National Forest lists Very High fire danger for the East Zone - Goosenest Ranger District and Very High fire danger for the West Zone.

Modoc camping alert lists 14-day stay limits and group-size limits, but the forest-order body shows a 2021-2023 effective range while the alert has no end date; confirm current camping rules directly before promotion.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Modoc National Forest Camping Restrictions alert

readinessEvidence.finding: Public GPX route source lists full route at 319 miles and 6 days, too long for July 10-13 as a full route.
Klamath lists Very High fire danger for East Zone Goosenest and West Zone; Modoc High fire danger also requires segment-level final refresh.

promotionActions.action: Resolve target weekend fit before ride-ready promotion.
